title: Jade Sabre
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}} {{Conflicting|''[[Survivor's Quest]]''|''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]''}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Jadesabre.jpg]] |name=''Jade Sabre'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' |line= |model= |type=[[Shuttle/Legends|Shuttle]] |cost=*Not available for sale''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' *Likely valued at 1,675,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |modifier= |sysmods= |length=50 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=Greater than 1,050 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' states that the ''Jade Sabre'' was faster than the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], which had a maximum speed of 1,050 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]], according to ''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''. |maneuverability= |engine=[[Ion engine/Legends|Ion drive]] (2) |hyperdrive=*Class 0.3''[[Survivor's generator/Legends|shield generator]] |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*Retractable [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannon]] turrets (4) *[[Tractor beam projector/Legends|Tractor beam projector]] (1) |complement=[[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighter]] (1) |bays= |escapepods= |crew=[[Pilot/Legends|Pilot]] (1) |skeleton= |passengers=15 |capacity=100 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] |cargohandling= |consumables=6 [[Standard month/Legends|months]] |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=[[Transport/Legends|Transport]] |commission= |destroyed=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]{{CSWECite|II|32|''Jade Sabre''}} |retired= |battles=*[[Expedition to Outbound Flight|Expedition to ''Outbound Flight'']] *[[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict]] *[[Battle of Helska IV]] |affiliation=[[New Jedi Order]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=[[Mara Jade Skywalker]] |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|The ''Jade Sabre'' can fly through a firestorm. She can blow a starfighter out of the sky and run circles around Star Destroyers. She's as good a shuttle as anyone will find, and better than anything ExGal will put together.|Mara Jade Skywalker|}} The '''''Jade Sabre''''' was a custom-built and modified [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] that was constructed by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] as a gift to [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], shortly after their [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|wedding]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. He gave her the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to replace the ''[[Jade's Fire]]'', her previous ship that Jade Skywalker sacrificed during their [[Mission to Nirauan|mission]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]]. The ''Jade Sabre'' was an fast, fish-head shaped [[Color/Legends|green]] starship equipped with military-grade [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] and concealed [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannon]] weaponry. Mara Jade Skywalker used the ship on a number of expeditions and battles, including an [[Expedition to Outbound Flight|expedition]] with the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] to investigate the wreckage of ''[[Outbound Flight]]'' in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. She later flew the ''Jade Sabre'' during the prelude to and early days of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] with the invading [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. This included a diplomatic mission with Luke's twin sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her daughter [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] to mediate the [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict|rising tensions]] between the [[Osarian (group)|Osarian]] and [[Rhommamoolian]] peoples. This conflict was secretly incited by the Yuuzhan Vong [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] [[Nom Anor]]. Later, the Skywalkers flew the ship to investigate [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]], another early site of Yuuzhan Vong sabotage. The outbreak of the war culminated with a [[Battle of Helska IV|battle]] at [[Helska IV]], the headquarters of the [[Praetorite Vong]]. She flew her ship in the battle with aid from her husband, Jaina Solo, and [[Danni Quee]], a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who was rescued from Yuuzhan Vong [[Prisoner/Legends|captivity]] by Jaina Solo's twin brother [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]]. Two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Battle of Helska IV, Mara Jade Skywalker flew the ''Jade Sabre'' to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] with her youngest nephew [[Anakin Solo]]. The expedition was meant to give Jade Skywalker rest and help her recover from the mysterious [[Coomb spore|illness]] that had plagued her since before the war. While on Dantooine, she and Solo were discovered by several Yuuzhan Vong. With their path to the ''Jade Sabre'' blocked, the pair embarked on a dangerous flight from the Yuuzhan Vong forces. Meanwhile, the [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] discovered the ship and destroyed it because of their religious hatred of mechanical technology, eliminating Solo and Jade Skywalker's only way off the planet. Some time later, Mara Jade Skywalker was gifted a replacement ship by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Tendra Risant Calrissian]], which was christened the ''[[Jade Shadow]]'' by Risant Calrissian. ==Description== {{Quote|Quick ship.''"
"''You think Luke would give Mara anything less?|Lando Calrissian and Han Solo|}} [[File:MaraLuke-SQcover.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Luke Skywalker designed the ''Jade Sabre'' for his wife, Mara Jade Skywalker.]] Designed and constructed by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] for his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], the ''Jade Sabre'' was a green [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] with a sleek fish-head shape swept-back tail fin wings, and flared side pods to protect its twin [[Ion engine/Legends|ion drives]]. An attractive [[Starship/Legends|starship]], even the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yomin Carr]], who hated mechanical technology as was typical of his [[Species/Legends|species]], acknowledged the ''Jade Sabre'' as one of the most beautiful he had ever seen. Only requiring a crew of one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], the vessel could also be operated by a team of two pilots or a single pilot with another individual handling the ship's main guns. Mara Jade Skywalker usually operated the ship herself and at other times teamed up with her husband to fly it. Because the ''Jade Sabre'' was special to her, Jade Skywalker was reluctant to allow others to fly it,''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' but often allowed [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], her niece and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]], to do so, a fact that made Solo's younger brother [[Anakin Solo|Anakin]] jealous. A civilian starship, the ''Jade Sabre'' was nonetheless equipped with military-grade technology, including its [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] and a primary [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] of a Class better than 0.3. In addition to the primary hyperdrive, it also had a backup Class 4 hyperdrive. The ship was fast in [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]], able to fly at speeds greater than the 1,050 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|km/h]] maxiumum of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], a ship that was regarded by some to be among the fastest in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].{{Film|V}} It also possessed multiple concealed weapons that only rose up from hidden turrets in combat situations. These included four [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]] and a [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|tractor beam projector]]. Despite its military grade equipment, the interior of the ''Jade Sabre'' was spacious and comfortable, possessing the amenities to accommodate up to 15 passengers across several rooms. Among these rooms were the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], a stateroom with a [[Sleeper (furniture)|bed]] that the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalkers]] shared, an expanded [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] for Luke's [[T-65XJ X-wing starfighter|XJ X-wing starfighter]],''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' and the space for up to 100 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo and six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] worth of consumable goods. As a unique custom-made ship, the ''Jade Sabre'' was not commercially available, but its estimated value was around 1,675,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. ==History== ===Construction and early use=== {{Quote|The ship is special to me. Your uncle Luke gave me the ''Jade Sabre'' to replace the ''Jade's Fire''.''"
"''But you crashed the ''Jade's Fire'' on purpose. You meant to.''"
"''I did, and had good reason to do that, but that doesnt mean … Your uncle Luke understood how much the ship meant to me. He knew ''what'' it meant to me. He respected what I had done in sacrificing the ''Fire''. He had the ''Sabre'' made for me to thank me.|Mara Jade Skywalker and Anakin Solo|}} [[File:JadesFire-CrackensThreatDossier.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The ''Jade Sabre'' was a gift to replace the ''Jade's Fire''. (pictured)]] In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] during her [[Mission to Nirauan|mission]] with Luke Skywalker to infiltrate the [[Hand of Thrawn]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]], Mara Jade sacrificed her previous starship, the ''[[Jade's Fire]]'', by deliberately crashing it into the fortress's [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]].''[[Vision of the Future]]'' Shortly after the pair were [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|married]] that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Luke Skywalker designed and built a replacement shuttle as a gift for his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]]. Jade Skywalker believed he had the ''Sabre'' built to thank her for sacrificing the ''Jade's Fire'' on Nirauan because he respected her sacrifice and was aware of how much it and the freedom it represented had meant to her. Jade Skywalker used the ship throughout the years that followed. In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], she embarked in the ''Jade Sabre'' on a mission to meet with several of her long-time employer [[Talon Karrde]]'s current and former associates. Her husband accompanied her on the journey, in hopes of spending time together between the meetings, something that had been difficult to come by in their first three years of marriage as busy [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] of the [[New Jedi Order]]. It was during this trip that they received word of a communication from the [[Empire of the Hand]] that sent them to [[Crustai]] on the edge of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], where they rendezvoused with the Chiss ship ''[[Chaf Envoy]]'', for a [[Expedition to Outbound Flight|journey]] to investigate the recently discovered remains of ''[[Outbound Flight]]''. Although they received the message later than intended, by using the ''Jade Sabre''{{'s}} high quality hyperdrive, they were able to reach Crustai the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] before the allotted meeting time was up. After arriving on the ''Chaf Envoy'', Jade Skywalker left the ''Sabre'' on the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] ship until she returned home after the expedition. ===Yuuzhan Vong War=== ====Early invasion==== {{Quote|Can't shoot what you can't catch.|Mara Jade Skywalker, on the superior speed of the ''Jade Sabre''|}} [[File:Jainax-wing.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Jaina Solo, who flew the ''Jade Sabre'' at the Osarian system]] Around the outbreak of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Jade Skywalker continued to fly the ''Jade Sabre''. Although she regularly traveled alone, she also flew with [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], her niece and Jedi apprentice, on many occasions. She rarely trusted others with her ship, but often allowed her sixteen-year-old niece to fly it because she was an excellent pilot, even at such a young age. That year, Mara flew the ''Sabre'' on a diplomatic mission to the [[Osarian system]] with Luke's twin sister [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her daughter [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] to mediate the [[Osarian-Rhommamool conflict|rising tensions]] between the peoples of [[Osarian]] and [[Rhommamool/Legends|Rhommamool]]. Organa Solo was to meet with the "[[Rhommamoolian]]" leader of the [[Red Knights of Life]], [[Nom Anor]], who was secretly a Yuuzhan Vong [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] trying to incite war between the twin planets. Displeased that the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] was meeting with the Rhommamoolian's first, the [[Osarian (group)|Osarians]] sent the [[Osarian First-Force]] under the command of the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Grappa]] to intercept the incoming ship and redirect its course to the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Osa-Prime]]. When they refused to comply with these demands, Grappa sent a group of [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] to force the ''Jade Sabre'' down. Although the enemy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were firing on them, the three women were determined to escape without firing back. After nearly breaking free of their pursuers, thanks to the skill of pilot Jaina Solo and the ''Jade Sabre''{{'s}} superior speed, the impetuous Jedi [[Wurth Skidder]] cut into the conflict in his XJ [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]]. Skidder shot down a pursuing Headhunter, allowing for an easier escape but unintentionally sabotaging Organa Solo's peacemaking attempts because of his decision to prioritize his own glory. The ''Sabre'' rendezvoused with the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Mediator-class battle cruiser|''Mediator''-class battle cruiser]] [[Mediator (Mediator-class)|''Mediator'']] and then proceeded to [[Redhaven]] on Rhommamool for the planned meeting with Anor. The meeting went poorly, as Anor had no intentions of negotiating or avoiding war, and the ''Jade Sabre'' left the [[Star system/Legends|system]] en-route to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Later, after traveling to [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion]] and learning from [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] of the potential trouble on [[Belkadan/Legends|Belkadan]], a planet that had fallen out of communications, Mara Jade and Luke Skywalker went in the ''Jade Sabre'' to investigate. After an easy flight there, they discovered that Belkadan was overtaken by noxious fumes and showing few signs of life, not even from the [[Extragalactic Society]]'s [[ExGal-4]] outpost that was based there. Braving the toxic clouds of [[Carbon dioxide/Legends|carbon dioxide]] and [[Methane/Legends|methane]], the ''Sabre'' entered the Belkadan's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], where it was met with violent winds and [[Electromagnetic field/Legends|electromagnetic]] imbalance that interfered with its [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] and instruments. After breaching the clouds, the ship encountered an air pocket that set it on a thousand [[Meter/Legends|meter]] drop straight down. When they reached the surface, the Skywalkers found both signs of Yuuzhan Vong biological sabotage and the perpetrator, Yomin Carr. Jade Skywalker [[Duel on Belkadan|fought]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Carr, after which they made an equally difficult exit from Belkadan in the ''Jade Sabre'' and returned to Dubrillion with their findings. ====Battle of Helska IV==== {{Quote|Get close between the ''Falcon'' and the ''Jade Sabre''. We'll take you in.|Luke Skywalker to Jaina Solo, during the Battle of Helska IV|}} [[File:Helska4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Jade Sabre'' flew in a space battle over Helska IV.]] Upon their arrival back at Dubrillion, the Skywalkers shared their findings of the Yuuzhan Vong activity on Dubrillion and learned of both their [[Battle of Dubrillion|attack on Dubrillion]] and the [[Praetorite Vong]]-engineered [[destruction of Sernpidal]] that killed their long-time friend [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. In response to these attacks on several fronts, the Jedi and New Republic forces gathered their began to plan a counterstrike on the Praetorite Vong base on [[Helska IV]]. The ''Jade Sabre'' traveled to the system alongside the ''Millennium Falcon'', the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Rejuvenator]]'', six [[Ranger-class gunship|''Ranger''-class gunships]], a starfighter unit and several other ships. They engaged the enemy [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]], until the Yuuzhan Vong destroyed the ''Rejuvenator'', forcing a retreat of the remaining New Republic forces. As they retreated, the ''Jade Sabre'', ''Millennium Falcon'' and ''[[Merry Miner]]'' flew down to Helska IV's surface in an attempt to extract [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], who had entered the Yuuzhan Vong base in an [[iceborer]] to rescue the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Danni Quee]] and Jedi Knight [[Miko Reglia]] before the outbreak of the space battle. The three ships successfully rescued Solo in the ''Merry Miner'' and rejoined the fleet as it regrouped to develop a new plan after the initial failure. The second assault used a group of [[shieldship]]s to reflect the energy of the Yuuzhan Vong's [[yammosk]] war coordinator back to Helska IV and make the world inhospitable to life. The ''Jade Sabre'' served as defense for the shieldships, with Jaina Solo piloting, Jade Skywalker as [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] and Quee offering aid anywhere she could. During the fight, Jade Skywalker lost consciousness because of an attack of the [[Coomb spore|mysterious illness]] she had long suffered. Temporarily distracted by her aunt's plight, the ''Jade Sabre'' was hit several times by opposing Yuuzhan Vong forces, depleting its shields and sending the ship out of control towards the planet below. Both Jade Skywalker's husband and Solo's [[Parent/Legends|mother]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|father]] took notice of the ''Sabre''{{'s}} descent. Acting quickly, Skywalker maneuvered his X-wing directly under the falling ship and fired his [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor coils]], providing the necessary propulsion for the shuttle to regain control and altitude. In time, the shieldships successfully rendered Helska IV so [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] that it killed both the [[yammosk]] and all members of the [[Praetorite Vong]] on its surface, and the ''Jade Sabre'' and other surviving ships retreated to Dubrillion after the victory. ====Destruction==== {{Quote|I wasn't going to fail you, Uncle Luke. I wasn't going to fail Aunt Mara. I just did what my mission required. I'm sorry I couldn't save the ''Sabre'' or the blasters and the other things we had on it.|Anakin Solo|}} [[File:Jade_Shadow.png|thumb|left|200px|Lando Calrissian gave Jade Skywalker the ''Jade Shadow'' as a gift after the ''Sabre''{{'s}} destruction.]] Two months after the Battle of Helska IV, Mara Jade Skywalker flew the ''Jade Sabre'' to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] with her youngest nephew Anakin Solo. With Jade Skywalker's health rapidly deteriorating from the mysterious Yuuzhan Vong-engineered disease, Luke Skywalker entrusted his wife's care to Solo. They hoped a period of rest on the remote Dantooine would help her recover after the difficult ordeals she had been through in the past months. Upon their arrival, Jade Skywalker insisted on piloting the ''Jade Sabre'' through its landing personally, trusting only herself with the ship. While Solo feared she had insisted on that because his aunt did not trust him, she revealed to him that the ship was special to her because it was a gift from her husband after her difficult sacrifice of the ''Jade's Fire''. Eventually, they encountered a group of Yuuzhan Vong, who cut them off from her ship. With their path to the shuttle blocked, the pair were forced to abandon it and embarked on a dangerous flight from the Yuuzhan Vong forces. Meanwhile, the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] warriors discovered the ship and destroyed it because of their religious hatred of mechanical technology, eliminating Solo and Jade Skywalker's only way off the planet. After a last minute rescue by Luke Skywalker and Solo's older brother Jacen, Anakin expressed his regret that he couldn't save the ''Jade Sabre''. Skywalker assured Solo that he did the best anyone could have expected and was grateful and proud of his apprentice's actions. In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], some time after the loss of the ''Sabre'', Jade Skywalker was gifted a new ship by her friend Lando Calrissian and his wife [[Tendra Risant Calrissian]]. The [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[Horizon-class star yacht|''Horizon''-class star yacht]] was christened ''[[Jade Shadow]]'' by Risant Calrissian, on account of its non-reflective gray colored hull.''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The ''Jade Sabre'' first appeared in the [[1999]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' by [[R. A. Salvatore]], the first installment of the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' series. It was first pictured in the [[2001]] [[West End Games]] [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' by [[Owen K.C. Stephens]]. Sources are inconsistent with regard to the Class of the ''Jade Sabre''{{'s}} hyperdrive. ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' indicates that the ''Jade Sabre'' could attain a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] speed of "somewhat better than 0.3 past lightspeed." Alternately, ''Starships of the Galaxy'' states that the ship's primary hyperdrive is Class 1, considerably slower given that a lower hyperdrive multiplier indicates a faster ship.''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' Despite the contradiction, Luke and Mara's ability to rendezvous with the Chiss at Crustai within the designated time limit in ''Survivor's Quest'' was dependent on the quality of the ''Jade Sabre''{{'s}} hyperdrive. ==Appearances== *''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' {{Ab|Survivor's Quest (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Survivor's Quest (abridged audiobook)|''Survivor's Quest'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]'' {{1st|[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|audiobook]]}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime'' abridged audiobook]] {{1st|''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' {{Mo}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught (abridged audiobook)|''The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught'' abridged audiobook]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|47|Mara Jade: Flesh and Blood}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' {{ID|''Jade's Sabre''}} *''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' {{1stp}} *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|27|MAR 1-4|Mara Jade Skywalker}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Jade Sabre |de=Jadeschwert |es=Sable de Jade }} [[Category:Shuttles]] [[Category:Starships of the New Jedi Order]]
